body:
Title: CRA Form T2203 2025 Part 3 Saskatchewan, Alberta, and British Columbia non-refundable tax credits.

Source:
- https://www.canada.ca/content/dam/cra-arc/formspubs/pbg/t2203/t2203-25e.pdf

CRA T2203 2025 Part 3 calculates provincial non-refundable tax credits for a multiple-jurisdiction return. For Saskatchewan, the form uses a basic personal amount of $19,491 on line 58040, a low credit rate of 10.5% encoded as 0.105, and a Schedule 9 line 14 high-rate credit rate of 14.5% encoded as 0.145. The Saskatchewan age amount worksheet line 58080 uses a maximum of $5,785, an income threshold of $43,066, and a 15% reduction rate encoded as 0.15. The spouse amount worksheet line 58120 uses a base amount of $21,440 and a maximum of $19,491. The eligible dependant worksheet line 58160 uses the same $21,440 base and $19,491 maximum. Saskatchewan Part 3 also includes infirm dependant amount, an amount for dependent children born in 2007 or later of $7,704 per child, a senior supplementary amount of $2,028, CPP or QPP contributions, enhanced CPP or QPP contributions, employment insurance premiums, enhanced EI premiums, a $3,000 volunteer firefighters, search and rescue volunteers, or volunteer emergency medical first responders amount, Schedule S12MJ amount, first-time home buyers' amount capped at $15,000, pension income capped at $1,000, caregiver and disability amounts, disability supports, Schedule S11 or S11MJ amounts, Schedule S2MJ amounts, and medical expenses. The Saskatchewan medical expense worksheet line ME subtracts the lesser of 3% of net income, encoded as 0.03, and $2,681 from eligible medical expenses.

For Alberta, the form uses a basic personal amount of $22,323 on line 58040, a low credit rate of 8% encoded as 0.08, a Schedule 9 line 13 credit rate of 60% encoded as 0.60, and a Schedule 9 line 14 high-rate credit rate of 21% encoded as 0.21. The Alberta age amount worksheet line 58080 uses a maximum of $6,221, an income threshold of $46,308, and a 15% reduction rate encoded as 0.15. The spouse amount worksheet line 58120 and eligible dependant worksheet line 58160 use a base amount of $22,323. Alberta Part 3 also includes infirm dependant amount, CPP or QPP contributions, enhanced CPP or QPP contributions, employment insurance premiums, enhanced EI premiums, adoption expenses capped at $19,354 per child, pension income capped at $1,719, caregiver and disability amounts, disability supports, Schedule S11 or S11MJ amounts, Schedule S2MJ amounts, and medical expenses. The Alberta medical expense worksheet line ME subtracts the lesser of 3% of net income, encoded as 0.03, and $2,884 from eligible medical expenses.

For British Columbia, the form uses a basic personal amount of $12,932 on line 58040 and a low credit rate of 5.06% encoded as 0.0506. The British Columbia age amount worksheet line 58080 uses a maximum of $5,799, an income threshold of $43,169, and a 15% reduction rate encoded as 0.15. The spouse amount worksheet line 58120 uses a base amount of $12,181 and a maximum of $11,073. The eligible dependant worksheet line 58160 uses the same $12,181 base and $11,073 maximum. British Columbia Part 3 also includes the British Columbia caregiver amount line 58175, CPP or QPP contributions, enhanced CPP or QPP contributions, employment insurance premiums, enhanced EI premiums, a $3,000 volunteer firefighters or search and rescue volunteers amount, adoption expenses capped at $19,580 per child, pension income capped at $1,000, disability amounts, disability supports, Schedule S11 or S11MJ amounts, student designated amounts, Schedule S2MJ amounts, medical expenses, donations and gifts, and the British Columbia farmers' food donation tax credit. The British Columbia medical expense worksheet line ME subtracts the lesser of 3% of net income, encoded as 0.03, and $2,689 from eligible medical expenses. The British Columbia donations and gifts worksheet line 58969 uses a first $200 donation amount, taxable income threshold of $259,829, a 20.5% high donation rate encoded as 0.205, a 16.8% middle donation rate encoded as 0.168, and the 5.06% low rate encoded as 0.0506. The British Columbia farmers' food donation tax credit is 25% of eligible qualifying gifts, encoded as 0.25.

For each province in this Part 3 batch, the encoded subtotal adds the basic personal amount, age amount, spouse amount, eligible dependant amount, selected listed credits, capped province-specific amounts, and disability-related lines. Saskatchewan total non-refundable tax credit line C multiplies the subtotal and later Part 3 lines by 10.5% and adds Schedule 9 line 14 at 14.5%. Alberta line C multiplies the subtotal and later Part 3 lines by 8%, then adds Schedule 9 line 13 at 60% and Schedule 9 line 14 at 21%. British Columbia line C multiplies the subtotal and later Part 3 lines by 5.06%, then adds donations and gifts line 58969 and the farmers' food donation tax credit line 58980. Per-dependant row tables for infirm dependants, BC caregivers, caregiver amounts, disability transfers, and medical expenses for other dependants, plus detailed S2, S11, and S12 transfer schedules, are not encoded in this focused module.
